output:
The answer is **(D) 36.7 kWh**.

Each cycle retains 99.5% of the **current** capacity (not the original), so after 200 cycles:

$$100 \times 0.995^{200} = 100 \times 0.3670 \approx 36.7 \text{ kWh}$$

The technician's error is treating compound degradation as linear. Since each 0.5% loss applies to an ever-shrinking base, the battery never reaches zero — it follows exponential decay.
